channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: A Look Back at Women’s Fashion in the 1970s
item.description: The 1970s were a transformative era in women’s fashion, blending self-expression, cultural shifts, and bold experimentation. Early in the decade, the influence of the late 1960s was still evident, wit...

item.title: Moderator Clarifies Macron's Brief Intervention at Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi
item.description: During the Africa Forward Summit held in Nairobi on 13 May 2026, a short moment involving French President Emmanuel Macron went viral online, but the full context has been misunderstood. According to ...

item.title: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome officially renamed PMOS to improve women’s healthcare
item.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is a hormonal disorder affecting around 10% to 13% of women of reproductive age, though many remain undiagnosed. The condition’s original name has been criticised for ...

item.title: Vaughan Temporarily Eliminates City Development Charges to Boost Housing
item.description: The City of Vaughan has implemented a historic measure to address housing affordability by temporarily reducing all city development charges (DCs) for shovel-ready residential projects to zero. Led by...

item.title: RSS Welcomes Suvendu Adhikari as Part of Ideological Family Without Formal Membership
item.description: In an exclusive statement, Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) General Secretary Dattatreya Hosabale expressed pride in Suvendu Adhikari, stating that he does not require a formal membership to be consi...

item.title: Government and Partners Support Irrigation Farming to Improve Rural Productivity
item.description: Dodoma — Farmers for Tanzania, especially those wey dey live for village areas, dey benefit well from strong collaboration between government and development partners. Eldad Barbar, Resident Director ...

item.title: University of Nebraska Develops Early Detection Test for Andes Hantavirus
item.description: As a rare Andes hantavirus outbreak occurred on a cruise ship, the United States faced a shortage of early diagnostic tools for the virus. The University of Nebraska Medical Center rapidly developed a...

item.title: FG Organises Nationwide Reading Relay to Promote Family Health and Break Guinness Record
item.description: The Federal Government through the Ministry of Youth and Social Development don dey prepare for big Guinness World Record attempt wey go involve over 300 participants to read together in a relay, hopi...

item.title: Man sentenced for attempted murder after shaking infant
item.description: A man has been sentenced to prison after violently shaking a baby girl, an act that authorities have classified as attempted murder. The incident caused serious injuries to the infant, who required ur...

item.title: US House Committee Seeks Instructure Testimony Following Multiple Canvas Data Breaches
item.description: U.S. House lawmakers are pressing Instructure, the company behind the Canvas educational platform, to provide detailed answers regarding recent cyberattacks that compromised the personal data of milli...

item.title: Nottingham attacks: Brother of killer Valdo Calocane tells public inquiry of fears over his mental health
item.description: The brother of Valdo Calocane, the perpetrator of the Nottingham attacks in June 2023, has given evidence at a public inquiry, revealing his fears that his sibling might take his own life. Elias Caloc...

item.title: Iran supports Indian efforts to promote peace in West Asia
item.description: Iran has expressed its support for any Indian initiative aimed at ending the ongoing war in West Asia and promoting regional security and stability. Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi h...

item.title: Kenya MPs Call for Government-Owned Offices to Reduce Lease Expenses
item.description: Kenyan MPs have raised concerns over the government's high expenditure on leasing and renovating office space, especially at a time when the State Department for Parliamentary Affairs faces budget cut...

item.title: The Beach Boys in the 1960s: Their Rise, Hits, and Musical Evolution
item.description: The Beach Boys, formed in 1961 in Hawthorne, California, became one of the most influential American bands of the 1960s. The core members included brothers Brian, Carl, and Dennis Wilson, their cousin...

item.title: LSK and Civil Society Challenge Cyber Surveillance Powers in Supreme Court
item.description: Nairobi — Kenya is witnessing a significant legal showdown over digital rights as the Law Society of Kenya (LSK), along with the Bloggers Association of Kenya, Article 19 Eastern Africa, and the Kenya...

item.title: Azerbaijan Designates 2026 for Urban Planning and Sustainable Architecture in Preparation for WUF13
item.description: The Republic of Azerbaijan has officially declared 2026 as the 'Year of Urban Planning and Architecture', coinciding with its hosting of the 13th World Urban Forum (WUF13) in Baku from 17–22 May 2026....

item.title: Tanzania to Build Modern Muhimbili National Hospital to Improve Healthcare Services
item.description: Tanzania MPs have welcomed the government’s plan to construct a new Muhimbili National Hospital (MNH), calling it a strategic and long-awaited investment for the country’s healthcare system. Valued at...

item.title: DHS Launches Audit of Warehouse-Based Immigration Detention Program Under Kristi Noem
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security's inspector general has initiated an audit into a controversial $38 billion program that converted commercial warehouses into immigration detention centers during K...

item.title: Inquiry into Nigel Farage’s £5m Gift and Financial Transparency
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, faces scrutiny over a £5 million gift from Thai-based crypto-billionaire Christopher Harborne, which was given in 2024 shortly before Farage’s decision to stand in t...

item.title: Three young women found dead in the sea off Brighton coast
item.description: The bodies of three young women, believed to be aged between 20 and 30, were recovered from the sea off the coast of Brighton on Wednesday morning. Sussex Police launched a search after reports of a p...
